question_title: What will be the value of "Euro Short-Term Rate: Volume-Weighted Trimmed Mean Rate" on 2026-04-09?
summary: The €STR (Euro Short-Term Rate) on April 9, 2026 will almost certainly remain very close to its current level of ~1.932%. The ECB deposit facility rate is at 2.0%, and the next monetary policy meeting is April 30 — well after the resolution date. No rate change is possible before April 9. The €STR has been in an extremely stable regime since June 2025, tracking 6-8bps below the deposit rate with a standard deviation of only 0.16bps in 2026. April 9 is a normal Thursday business day with no month-end effects, holidays, or maintenance period boundaries. The distribution is therefore very tight, centered on 1.932 with nearly all probability mass between 1.929 and 1.935.

factors.description: ECB deposit facility rate is 2.0% and next monetary policy meeting is April 30 — no rate change possible before April 9. The €STR tracks ~7-8bps below the deposit rate.
€STR has been in an extremely stable regime since June 2025 with std=0.004. In 2026 specifically, the mean is 1.932 with std=0.0016 (excluding month-end dips).
April 9 is a normal Thursday TARGET2 business day — not month-end, not a holiday (Easter Monday was April 6), and mid-maintenance period. No special effects expected.
Small tail risk from Iran war uncertainty per ECB March statement could theoretically trigger emergency ECB action or money market stress. But ECB held rates twice already despite this.
Latest value (March 31) was 1.929, very slightly below the 2026 mean of 1.932. Minor mean reversion expected but within normal noise.

reasoning:
**Forecast: €STR on April 9, 2026 ≈ 1.932%**

Key reasoning:
- The ECB deposit facility rate is 2.0%, unchanged since June 2025, and the **next monetary policy meeting is April 30** — after the resolution date
- The €STR has been extraordinarily stable in 2026, with a standard deviation of only **0.16 basis points** and all non-month-end values between 1.929 and 1.936
- April 9 is a normal Thursday business day with no calendar effects (Easter Monday was April 6; no month-end; mid-maintenance period)
- Monte Carlo simulation using the empirical 2026 distribution plus small tail scenarios for emergency ECB actions confirms a very tight distribution centered on 1.932

The 80% confidence interval is [1.930, 1.934] — reflecting the near-certainty that this rate remains anchored to the unchanged ECB policy rate.
sources_consulted: [ECB Deposit Facility Rate for Euro Area (ECBDFR) | FRED | St. Louis Fed](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/ECBDFR)
[ECB Interest Rates: April 2026 Trading Odds & Predictions | Polymarket](https://polymarket.com/event/ecb-interest-rates-april-2026)
resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series ECBESTRVOLWGTTRMDMNRT once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=ECBESTRVOLWGTTRMDMNRT`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-04-09 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.
